reasoning: The historical base rate of the S&P 500 decreasing by over 5% in a single day within a given year is roughly 11% (this has occurred in 8 to 9 years out of the last 74 years since 1950, primarily clustered in crisis years such as 1987, 2008, and 2020). The freeze value for this market is ~11.2%, which lines up perfectly with this historical base rate. Without any certain indicators that 2025 will have abnormally high volatility compared to the base rate, the safest and most accurate prediction remains aligned with historical averages and the market equilibrium.
